detail.title: Cross-Cutting Rules, Interface Contracts, and Non-Functional Requirements

requirement.title: R1 — Human Consent for Raw Source
requirement.description: Design docs (raw source) MUST NOT be created or modified without human consent or initiation. The LLM assists in elicitation but the human controls when a design doc is committed.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A design doc MUST NOT be written to disk until the user explicitly confirms the content (e.g., "looks good", "commit it", section-by-section approval). REQ-F024
After a design doc is committed, it MUST NOT be modified. Any change requires a new iteration (new date folder, new design.md). REQ-F024

requirement.title: R2 — Unidirectional Flow
requirement.description: Downstream stages MUST NOT modify upstream artifacts. Refiner cannot modify design docs; planner cannot modify spec.xml.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Refiner MUST NOT write to any file under docs/plans/. Its only output path on success is specs/<spec-id>/. On block, refiner writes no files anywhere — the error is reported to terminal and refiner exits non-zero (per fr-rf-001-ac3). REQ-F025
Planner MUST NOT write to specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml or specs/<spec-id>/details/. Its only output paths are specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml and specs/<spec-id>/epics/. REQ-F025

requirement.title: Design Doc Contract
requirement.description: The design doc is the complete output of brainstorming and the complete input to the refiner. The refiner consumes the entire design doc — no special section or structured summary is needed. This contract defines the design doc structure with context-sensitive sections: when no prior spec exists, the doc carries full problem/solution/requirements/scope in prose; when a prior spec exists, the doc carries Context + Change Intent. There are no modes — one design doc structure with conditional sections.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Design docs MUST be located at docs/plans/<spec-id>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/design.md. The spec-id is derived from the directory name. The iteration date is derived from the date directory name. REQ-F003

When no prior spec exists for this spec-id, the design doc MUST contain substantive design content: at minimum, a description of the problem, proposed solution, and identifiable requirements (in prose). Empty or stub design docs are ERROR. REQ-F003

When a prior spec exists for this spec-id, the design doc MUST include at minimum: a Context section (summarizing current spec state with version reference) and a Change Intent section (describing what is changing and why). Missing Context or Change Intent is ERROR. A pre-authored structured delta section (ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED lists) MUST NOT appear — the refiner derives the delta from narrative intent. REQ-F005

The refiner determines which conditional sections to expect from filesystem state, not from fields in the design doc: specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists → expect Context + Change Intent; does not exist → expect prose with problem/solution/requirements/scope. This is a single-behavior filesystem check, not a mode selector. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Contract
requirement.description: The spec.xml identity header is the interface contract between refiner (producer) and planner (consumer). This is the single source of truth for the header format.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Identity header MUST contain: spec_id (string), spec_version (positive integer), status ("active"), source/design_path (valid file path), source/design_iteration (YYYY-MM-DD). Missing fields are ERROR. REQ-F010
source/design_path MUST point to an existing design doc file. A path pointing to a non-existent file is ERROR. REQ-F010
scope MUST list included features with IDs and excluded items with reasons. Empty scope is WARNING. REQ-F010
When spec_version > 1, supersedes MUST be set to "<spec-id>:v<previous-version>". Missing supersedes for v2+ is ERROR. REQ-F010

requirement.title: SDD Schemas (Guidance Layer)
requirement.description: Each pipeline artifact MUST have a corresponding schema file in scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ that serves as the format guide for LLMs. Schema files are the guidance layer — they tell the LLM what format to produce. They live in scripts/lib/ (shared infrastructure) so any skill can read them without cross-skill coupling. Full schema content definitions are in details/sdd-schemas.xml (fr-sd-001 through fr-sd-007).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/design.md MUST exist and conform to the design doc schema definition (fr-sd-001 through fr-sd-004). REQ-F003, REQ-F005
scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/spec.md MUST exist and conform to the spec identity header schema definition (fr-sd-005 through fr-sd-007). REQ-F010
Schema files are the guidance layer only. They MUST NOT be the enforcement mechanism — enforcement is delegated to sdd-utils.js (fr-cc-if-004). REQ-F008

requirement.title: SDD Utils (Validation Toolkit)
requirement.description: Deterministic validation and information checks MUST be implemented in scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js. This module is a validation toolkit — it validates structure and provides information, but does NOT merge or author spec content (the LLM manages spec content directly, like maintaining a wiki page). Full API definitions are in details/sdd-schemas.xml (fr-sd-008, fr-sd-009). LLM judgment is reserved for semantic checks (contradictions, ambiguity).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: sdd-utils.js MUST export the design doc validator API defined in fr-sd-008 (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc). REQ-F003, REQ-F005
sdd-utils.js MUST export the spec header validator API defined in fr-sd-009 (parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader). REQ-F010, REQ-F016
Semantic checks NOT implemented by this module (delegated to LLM): contradiction detection between requirements, ambiguity assessment, scope completeness judgment. REQ-F008, REQ-F015
When a schema file (fr-cc-if-003) and sdd-utils.js disagree on what is valid, sdd-utils.js wins. Schema files guide production; sdd-utils.js enforces correctness. REQ-F003, REQ-F010

requirement.title: Skill Access Pattern
requirement.description: Each pipeline skill accesses the contract through two layers: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ for format guidance (what to produce) and scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js for programmatic validation (is it correct). Both layers are shared infrastructure. No skill reads another skill's files.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: arc-brainstorming SKILL.md MUST invoke scripts/lib/print-schema.js (with target "design") before producing the design doc, to obtain the canonical schema derived from DESIGN_DOC_RULES. The skill MUST NOT embed a structural template for the design doc — structural content is schema, and schema has a single source (fr-sd-010, fr-sd-011). REQ-F003

arc-refining SKILL.md MUST invoke scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js validateDesignDoc() for deterministic input validation before any formalization. If valid is false with ERROR issues, refiner MUST block per fr-rf-001-ac3 — print the issues to terminal, exit non-zero, and write no files (no refiner-report.md, no spec.xml). REQ-F008

arc-refining SKILL.md MUST invoke scripts/lib/print-schema.js (with target "spec") when producing spec.xml, and MUST invoke scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js validateSpecHeader() for deterministic output validation. If valid is false, refiner MUST NOT write any files. The skill MUST NOT embed a structural template for the spec header — structural content comes from the CLI and the exported SPEC_HEADER_RULES constant. REQ-F010, REQ-F015

arc-planning SKILL.md MUST invoke scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js validateSpecHeader() for spec.xml input validation before any decomposition. Planner MUST NOT read the design doc — it works from the spec only. Rationale: the three-layer model requires that the derived layer (DAG) reads only from the wiki layer (spec), never from the raw source layer (design doc). The spec's <delta> metadata provides the planning scope that would otherwise require reading the design doc. REQ-F016

requirement.title: Validation Toolkit API Self-Documentation
requirement.description: The exported function signatures of scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc, parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader) reveal the expected input/output schemas through their destructuring patterns and return types. This exposure is intentional — it makes the validation toolkit self-documenting for both human maintainers and LLM agents exploring the codebase. Any consumer reading the module can infer schema requirements (spec_id, spec_version, supersedes, delta structure, etc.) without reading sdd-schemas/ files. This property has testing-methodology implications documented in nfr-003.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A consumer (human or LLM agent) reading scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST be able to identify the expected fields of a spec identity header (spec_id, spec_version, supersedes, delta structure, added/modified/removed/renamed children) from the parseSpecHeader return destructuring alone, without consulting any external documentation. REQ-F010

When sdd-utils.js function signatures change (adding, removing, or renaming returned fields), the format contracts in scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ MUST be updated in the same iteration. The two layers describe the same underlying schema — sdd-utils enforces it, sdd-schemas documents it. Drift between them is ERROR. REQ-F010

requirement.title: _pending-conflict.md Handoff Schema
requirement.description: The _pending-conflict.md file is the interface contract between refiner (producer, on R3 axis-1/2/3 block per fr-rf-015) and brainstorming (consumer, in Phase 0 iterate-branch auto-entry per fr-bs-008). This contract defines the file's location, lifecycle, and required content fields. Per A.1's narrowed Iron Law clause 3, this artifact is the explicit ephemeral exception to "no authoritative state on block" — non-versioned, non-authoritative, and consumed-and-deleted by brainstorming on successful new-design write.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: File location MUST be specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md. The underscore prefix marks the file as ephemeral hand-off, not versioned spec content. The file MUST NOT live under details/ or any path that would be parsed as a spec detail file. 2026-04-27:B.2

The file MUST carry: (i) which axis fired (1, 2, or 3); (ii) the conflict description (specific design line ranges and Q&A row q_ids involved); (iii) 1–3 candidate resolutions, each phrased as a concrete action the user can pick; (iv) a user-action prompt directing the user to /arc-brainstorming iterate <spec-id>. 2026-04-27:B.2

Lifecycle: refiner writes the file on R3 axis-1/2/3 block per fr-rf-015; brainstorming reads it as Change Intent seed in Phase 0 per fr-bs-008; brainstorming deletes it on successful new-design write. The file MUST NOT persist across a completed conflict cycle — net zero authoritative state preserved. 2026-04-27:B.2, 2026-04-27:B.3

The file MUST be machine-parseable so that brainstorming Phase 0 can mechanically iterate over candidate resolutions and the cited design line ranges / Q&A q_ids without LLM re-interpretation. The wire format (YAML, structured markdown, etc.) is an implementation choice; the contract is the field shape and machine-parseability. 2026-04-27:B.2

requirement.title: Structured Decision-Log Format
requirement.description: The decision-log is the interface contract between brainstorming (producer of Q&A rows during Phase 2 elicitation per fr-bs-009) and refiner (consumer for Phase 4 axis 2 contradiction check and Phase 6 mechanical authorization check per fr-rf-010-ac5). This contract defines the field shape and machine-parseability. The wire format (YAML, strict markdown table, etc.) is a deliberately unspecified implementation choice — pinning the format here would over-constrain the implementation and freeze a swappable detail into the contract.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each Q&A row MUST carry exactly four required fields: q_id (stable identifier, unique per brainstorming session), question (verbatim text of the question asked), user_answer_verbatim (verbatim text of the user's answer), and deferral_signal (boolean). Missing any field is ERROR. 2026-04-27:B.1

The decision-log MUST be machine-parseable. The producer (brainstorming) and consumer (refiner) MUST agree on a single wire format — drift between producer and consumer is ERROR. The format choice is an implementation decision; the spec captures the contract (field shape, parseability), not the format. 2026-04-27:B.1

Rows MUST be addressable by q_id so that refiner's Phase 6 mechanical authorization check (per fr-rf-010-ac5) can iterate over rows and verify cited content appears in the correct row's user_answer_verbatim. Lookup by q_id MUST be a deterministic operation on the parsed structure. 2026-04-27:B.1, 2026-04-27:B.4

The deferral_signal field MUST be set to true when the user_answer_verbatim matches one of the canonical deferral phrases ("use defaults", "covered.", "skip", "you decide", and similar) per fr-bs-009-ac4. deferral_signal=true tells refiner the corresponding axis is unbound — refiner MUST NOT treat it as authorization for a concrete MUST per fr-rf-013. 2026-04-27:B.1

requirement.title: Skill Path Discipline CI Lint
requirement.description: cc-005 (Cross-Component Plugin Path Discipline, defined in spec.xml <cross_cutting>) requires LLM-facing prose in skills/, templates/, and agents/ to prefix references to plugin shared library content (paths under scripts/lib/) with ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/. This requirement specifies the mechanical enforcement: a CI lint test that scans the affected files syntactically and fails on bare scripts/lib/ references. The lint scope is deliberately narrow per design.md's path-resolution axis — Node.js module-system paths inside scripts/lib/, test runner paths inside tests/, and hook paths via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} are out-of-scope because each has a separate, correct mechanism. Skill-local relative patterns are author's judgment and not enforced.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: tests/scripts/skill-path-discipline.test.js MUST exist and scan three globs: skills/**/SKILL.md, templates/**/*.md, agents/**/*.md. The scan MUST detect two violation patterns: (i) bare scripts/lib/ references in prose not preceded by ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/ on the same logical token, and (ii) node -e "require('./scripts/lib/...')" cwd-relative require invocations. Detection MUST use regex patterns documented inline in the test file with rationale comments. design.md:Path-resolution axis section

Each violation MUST report: file path, line number, the offending line content (truncated to 120 chars if longer), and the corrective form (${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/scripts/lib/...). Test failure MUST block CI and merge — there is no bypass annotation, no skip marker, no allowlist mechanism. Remediation is to fix the path; suppression is forbidden. design.md:Path-resolution axis section

The lint MUST NOT scan: scripts/lib/ (Node module system handles cross-project safety via file-relative resolution), tests/ (test runner sets cwd to plugin root), hooks/ (hooks use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} per Claude Code plugin contract), and skill-local paths under skills/<name>/scripts/ or skills/<name>/references/ that use ${SKILL_ROOT}/ or cd-then-bare patterns. Static analysis cannot reliably distinguish legitimate from broken cd-then-bare patterns without parsing Bash semantics; cc-005 leaves these to author's judgment per fr-cc-pl-001-ac1's narrowed scope. design.md:Path-resolution axis section

requirement.title: Existing P2/P3 Violation Remediation
requirement.description: Landing fr-cc-pl-001 with existing violations unfixed would ship a broken state — the new lint would fail on the existing audited violations. This requirement specifies that the audited P2/P3 violations across the affected SDD skills MUST be fixed in the same v3 sprint as fr-cc-pl-001's introduction. The fix is mechanical (add ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/ prefix); no semantic changes to SDD skill behavior are permitted under this requirement.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All existing bare scripts/lib/ references in skills/**/SKILL.md, templates/**/*.md, and agents/**/*.md MUST be prefixed with ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/ such that fr-cc-pl-001-ac1's lint passes cleanly when v3 is merged. The audit set includes (but is not limited to) arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, arc-planning; the lint surfaces the complete current set authoritatively. design.md:Path-resolution axis section

Fixes MUST be mechanical prefix additions only. Reorganizing Bash blocks, switching from CLI to direct read (or vice versa), changing skill instruction semantics, or any other non-prefix modification is out-of-scope for this requirement and MUST be tracked separately if needed. design.md:Path-resolution axis section

Pre-existing arc-writing-skills Path Resolution section (committed at 2a189db prior to v3 sprint start) is considered upstream pre-work and is not subject to this requirement's audit — it was authored against the v3 cc-005 contract from the outset. Any drift from the contract surfaced in that section MUST be fixed under this requirement. design.md:Architecture Impact section

requirement.title: Two-Pass Write Pattern
requirement.description: Stages that produce file artifacts (refiner, planner) MUST follow the two-pass write pattern: (1) build output in memory, (2) validate output against the relevant interface contract, (3) write to disk only if validation passes. This prevents partially written or invalid artifacts from reaching the filesystem.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Refiner MUST NOT write spec.xml or details/ files until the complete output has been built in memory and validated against fr-cc-if-002. REQ-F012, REQ-F015
Planner MUST NOT write dag.yaml or epics/ until the complete output has been built in memory and validated for structural integrity. REQ-F017
When output validation fails, the stage MUST report all ERROR findings with remediation guidance and produce no output files. REQ-F008, REQ-F015

requirement.title: Validation Report with Remediation
requirement.description: When a stage blocks due to validation failure, the report MUST include not just what failed but how to fix it. This follows OpenSpec's enriched error message pattern.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each ERROR finding MUST include: the specific field or section that failed, the expected format (referencing the interface contract), and a concrete remediation suggestion. REQ-F009
WARNING findings MUST be reported to the user but MUST NOT block the stage from producing output. REQ-F015

requirement.title: Path-Agnostic Skill Descriptions
requirement.description: Pipeline skill descriptions (frontmatter) MUST NOT hardcode artifact paths. Triggering conditions MUST describe intent and context, not file locations. The user provides the spec-id or target via their prompt.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, and arc-planning descriptions MUST NOT contain literal paths like "specs/spec.xml" or "specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml". They MUST describe triggering conditions in terms of user intent (e.g., "when converting design documents to structured specs"). REQ-N001
Each pipeline skill MUST accept a spec-id from the user's prompt as the primary routing input. The skill resolves the spec-id to the correct artifact paths internally. REQ-N001
When the user does not provide a spec-id, the skill MUST scan specs/ and docs/plans/ to present available targets and ask the user to choose. REQ-N001

requirement.title: Existing Infrastructure Compatibility
requirement.description: The SDD pipeline MUST work with arcforge's existing CLI and skill infrastructure. No new runtime dependencies.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All pipeline operations MUST be invocable via existing arcforge skill mechanism (arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, arc-planning) or their updated versions. REQ-N001
No new npm packages MUST be added to package.json dependencies. devDependencies are exempt. REQ-N001

requirement.title: File-Based Artifacts
requirement.description: All artifacts MUST be file-based (YAML, XML, Markdown). No database, no server, no external state store.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Pipeline state MUST be fully derivable from files on disk. Running `ls` and `cat` on the specs/ and docs/plans/ directories MUST be sufficient to determine the complete pipeline state. REQ-N002
No pipeline operation MUST require network access, a running server, or a database connection. REQ-N002

requirement.title: Skill Adherence Evaluation Methodology
requirement.description: SDD pipeline skills (arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, arc-planning) cannot be reliably evaluated via A/B comparison (baseline vs treatment) on format-correctness output. The self-documenting nature of sdd-utils.js (fr-cc-if-006) means a competent baseline agent can infer the correct output format by reading the module, closing the delta between baseline and treatment. This property was empirically confirmed during the 2026-04-17 eval session against Claude Sonnet 4.x and Opus 4.x models.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A/B evaluation of SDD pipeline skills MAY yield delta near zero on specific eval shapes — notably, iteration-happy-path cases where a valid prior spec.xml is pre-staged in the sandbox and the baseline agent can mimic its schema. This is a consequence of model capability combined with fr-cc-if-006, not a skill deficiency. The 2026-04-19 eval session empirically confirmed this zero-delta pattern on arc-refining v2-iteration-happy-path (with v1 fixture pre-staged) while SIMULTANEOUSLY finding large positive deltas on first-formalization and scope-discipline evals (arc-refining v1-first-formalization: +62 pts; arc-planning v1-plan-all-requirements: +29 pts; arc-brainstorming pressure-skip-exploration: +80 pts). Eval designers MUST NOT interpret a specific zero-delta case as "skill adds no value" and MUST NOT generalize it to all SDD evals. REQ-N001

The preferred eval form for SDD pipeline skills is single-condition agent evaluation (pass@k on treatment trials only), measuring reliability across k trials rather than behavioral delta against a non-discriminative baseline. SHIP threshold: pass rate >= 80% across k >= 5 trials after accounting for harness noise (grader failures, max_turns truncation). REQ-N001

Behaviors that CAN be discriminatively A/B tested for these skills are those NOT inferable from sdd-utils.js source alone — examples include: refiner DAG completion gate blocking behavior (fr-rf-012), terminal block message structure on contradiction (fr-rf-001-ac4), delta accumulation discipline (fr-rf-011-ac4), error recovery ordering, block-vs-proceed decisions. These remain candidates for focused A/B eval when discrimination is required. REQ-N001
